bull.b: Sustained multi-year top-line growth paired with very high product-level gross margins indicates durable unit economics for Mirum’s marketed bile-acid therapies. Strong gross margins give the company leverage to fund commercial expansion and R&D without immediate margin pressure, supporting long-term scalability as new

Mid-50% cash contribution margins signal strong cash profitability per unit sold for LIVMARLI/related products. bear.b: Trailing-twelve-month cash outflows signal decreased internal funding capacity despite recent sales growth. Renewed cash burn increases dependence on external financing to support R&D, launches, and commercial expansion, raising execution risk if capital markets tighten or if expected near-term approvals/sales underper

Material increase in leverage compared with earlier years reduces financial flexibility and raises solvency risk if operating losses persist. Higher debt loads can constrain strategic choices, increase refinancing or covenant exposure, and amplify dilution pressure when convertible instruments are used to shore up fund

bear.t: Large Acquisition-related Charge & Delayed GAAP Profit
bear.b: A massive one-time acquisition charge materially inflated quarterly operating costs and obscures core operating trends.
